title: Python generated CLI completion
larger_intended_outcome: Complete the Python side of #892 by making modules own behavior meaning as IR/contracts/product data only, making command-generation own reusable primitive execution machinery and target renderers, and making generated/python own the shipped executable Python CLI projection. Package behavior changes should be possible by editing implementation-independent artifacts and regenerating outputs, without hand-editing shipped module executable code.
non_goals: Do not claim weak-agent-safe adapter maturity is full Python generated CLI completion.
Do not treat a generated non-Python CLI that shells into Python package internals as target completion unless the dependency is explicitly accepted as a transitional runtime bridge.

Do not move live workspace inspection, mutation orchestration, or package-specific runtime primitive implementation into generated code until the operation contracts make the target-runtime boundary explicit.
Do not create a broad general-purpose language when a small JSON-Schema-backed operation IR plus thin target executors is sufficient.
Do not advance Bash, PowerShell, or TypeScript in this Python lane except where shared contracts must remain implementation-independent.

candidate_lanes.title: JSON Schema operation IR
candidate_lanes.outcome: A small JSON-Schema-backed operation IR exists for CLI-visible package behavior over Markdown, TOML, JSON, schemas, templates, managed files, and emitted output.
candidate_lanes.proof: Schema validation and one representative planning or memory operation prove the IR can describe behavior without relying on package-local CLI code as the source of truth.

candidate_lanes.title: Python runtime handler boundary
candidate_lanes.outcome: Generated Python dispatch can identify supported operation ids from IR while runtime operation handlers expose an explicit contract boundary that can be implemented or bridged by future targets without relying on package-local cli.py ownership.
candidate_lanes.proof: Generated package static proof plus Python conformance prove operation dispatch is generated, runtime handlers are behind an explicit operation contract, and any Python-runtime bridge assumption is visible rather than implicit. Root, planning, and memory executable front doors now route through generated_cli_entrypoint modules.

candidate_lanes.title: Python package-resource operation IR migration
candidate_lanes.outcome: Simple package-resource generated Python commands consume checked-in operation contracts and target-executor primitives instead of compatibility runtime handlers.
candidate_lanes.proof: Active execplan .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/python-operation-ir-package-resource-commands.plan.json must prove contract tooling, generated Python conformance, generated package freshness, and structured inventory before this lane is complete.

candidate_lanes.title: Python handwritten parser retirement for generated commands
candidate_lanes.outcome: Commands covered by command_package_ir no longer require duplicate handwritten argparse definitions in package-local cli.py files; hand-owned runtime helpers live in _runtime_cli.py modules while executable front doors route through generated_cli_entrypoint.
candidate_lanes.proof: Static proof rejects duplicated handwritten parser ownership for generated command/interface surfaces; Python black-box conformance still passes; repository inventory has no cli.py files outside generated Python surfaces.

candidate_lanes.title: Python command/interface change gate
candidate_lanes.outcome: Future package command/interface changes are forced through contracts, IR, generators, regenerated outputs, and black-box conformance rather than package-local executable cli.py edits.
candidate_lanes.proof: Proof selection and static generated-package checks fail when command/interface changes bypass contract or generated surfaces.

candidate_lanes.title: Python shipped module executable source retirement
candidate_lanes.outcome: Shipped module source trees, including the root module, no longer own executable CLI front doors, parser/help construction, command dispatch/fallback envelopes, generated-command runtime dispatch, or generic deterministic file/data/schema/template/output primitive execution.
candidate_lanes.proof: Active execplan must prove behavior-level source executable ownership rather than filename-only inventory: generated-package static checks reject full completion while executable markers remain in shipped module src, Python black-box and Docker conformance pass, and remaining module-local Python is either non-shipped generation support or explicit non-complete product-domain residue.

candidate_lanes.title: Python generated CLI completion promotion
candidate_lanes.outcome: The command-package IR can promote Python from adapter-layer-proven-not-full-generated-cli to full generated CLI completion only after parser, dispatch, interface, proof, runtime-handler boundaries, codegen-owned generic primitive execution, and shipped module source executable retirement are all proven.
candidate_lanes.proof: Generated package static proof, Python Docker conformance, root CLI authority, closeout trust, package planning summary, and codegen-owned primitive executor conformance all agree that original Python generated-CLI intent is satisfied.

candidate_lanes.title: Python codegen-owned primitive executor migration
candidate_lanes.outcome: Generic deterministic CLI-visible behavior over files, JSON, TOML, Markdown sections, schemas, templates, managed payloads, and JSON text emission moves from module-local runtime helpers into codegen-owned primitive executors where practical.
candidate_lanes.proof: Primitive conformance and generated Python black-box conformance prove representative operations execute through codegen-owned primitives without importing module-local CLI/runtime internals except explicitly inventoried package-specific judgment.

dependency_assumptions: Package-specific runtime judgment may remain hand-owned in the Python lane only after it is separated from CLI ownership behind explicit operation contracts; generic deterministic file/data/template/schema/output behavior must move toward codegen-owned primitives before full completion is claimed.
Future non-Python targets must either implement the same operation contracts or use an explicitly accepted transitional bridge; an implicit call into Python internals is not completion.
JSON Schema is sufficient as the contract layer for command packages, operation plans, payloads, and conformance fixtures; execution semantics belong to a small documented primitive set.
Generated Python parser and dispatch should consume command_package_ir and conformance contracts as the single source of truth.
Each lane must keep Python work independent from Bash and PowerShell transport promotion.
parallelization_assumptions: The first two Python lanes are ordered because parser retirement depends on a clearer runtime-operation boundary.
Interface-change gating can be explored in parallel only after the parser retirement boundary is explicit.
proof_expectations: Each promoted Python lane must include Python black-box conformance and Docker-backed Python conformance unless the lane is planning-only.
Completion proof must distinguish generated CLI transport, runtime operation contract coverage, and any target bridge dependency instead of treating them as one surface.
Closeout must run or inspect closeout_trust before claiming Python completion.

The command-package IR python_cli_completion.current_state may be full-generated-cli-complete only with a schema-backed python-only completion gate, static proof for all prerequisite evidence ids, codegen-owned primitive executor proof for generic deterministic behavior, and source-executable ownership proof that shipped module src no longer owns executable CLI/runtime implementation.
promotion_rule: Promote only the next ready Python lane with clear generated-output ownership, no Bash/PowerShell scope, and proof that implementation-independent artifacts remain authoritative.
notes: Created after #921 showed that adapter maturity was incorrectly treated as Python generated-CLI completion. Updated after user clarification that non-Python generated CLI targets are not meant to depend implicitly on Python internals. #930 established the small JSON-Schema-backed operation IR boundary with memory.list-files.report as the representative portable command. #931 promoted memory.list-files to runtime-consumed operation IR and opened #933 for remaining command groups. #933 is closed for the previous migration slice: every generated Python operation id is inventoried as runtime-consumed or accepted-hand-owned-runtime-primitive, with compatibility-runtime-handler rejected. The 2026-05-14 #892 clarification sharpened the finish line: accepted hand-owned runtime primitives are transitional debt, not full generated CLI completion, when they contain generic deterministic file/data/schema/template/output behavior that should belong to codegen-owned primitive executors. Root, planning, and memory executable front doors have been moved off package-local cli.py files and through generated_cli_entrypoint modules while runtime helpers remained in _runtime_cli.py. #954 closed the root runtime dispatch and completion-contract blocker in the base branch via PR #956; that implementation is necessary progress but not sufficient for #892 completion. The Python-scoped #892 lane is therefore reopened/demoted to partially-promoted, with TypeScript generated-resource completion still tracked separately by #929 and Bash/PowerShell remaining out of scope until re-promoted. 2026-05-14 planning repair: the active python-cli-src-executable-cleanup execplan is now part of this decomposition as the python-module-source-executable-retirement lane. The first implementation slice demotes command_package_ir python_cli_completion back to adapter-layer-proven-not-full-generated-cli and adds a behavior-marker static gate so full completion cannot be claimed while shipped module src still owns executable CLI/runtime behavior. The follow-up implementation slices moved generated package bridges, operation IR executors, and the remaining root/planning/memory runtime CLI modules into agentic_command_generation, leaving shipped module src without cli.py, _runtime_cli.py, generated_cli_package.py, or operation_ir_executor.py surfaces. The miss where implementation started before this decomposition was updated is routed as a dogfooding issue.
